children.heading: .—Fees Payable to Administrator
children.label: a .—Fees Payable to Administrator
children.text: All fees imposed and amounts collected under this chapter for services performed, or materials furnished, by the Federal Aviation Administration are payable to the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: .—Refunds
children.label: b .—Refunds
children.text: The Administrator may refund any fee paid by mistake or any amount paid in excess of that required.
shall be credited to a separate account established in the Treasury and made available for Administration activities;
shall be available immediately for expenditure but only for congressionally authorized and intended purposes; and
shall remain available until expended.
a list of fee collections by the Administration during the preceding fiscal year;
a list of activities by the Administration during the preceding fiscal year that were supported by fee expenditures and appropriations;
budget plans for significant programs, projects, and activities of the Administration, including out-year funding estimates;
any proposed disposition of surplus fees by the Administration; and
such other information as those committees consider necessary.

children.heading: .—Development of Cost Accounting System
children.label: e .—Development of Cost Accounting System
children.text: The Administration shall develop a cost accounting system that adequately and accurately reflects the investments, operating and overhead costs, revenues, and other financial measurement and reporting aspects of its operations.

children.heading: .—Compensation to Carriers for Acting as Collection Agents
children.label: f .—Compensation to Carriers for Acting as Collection Agents
children.text: The Administration shall prescribe regulations to ensure that any air carrier required, pursuant to the Air Traffic Management System Performance Improvement Act of 1996 or any amendments made by that Act, to collect a fee imposed on another party by the Administrator may collect from such other party an additional uniform amount that the Administrator determines reflects the necessary and reasonable expenses (net of interest accruing to the carrier after collection and before remittance) incurred in collecting and handling the fee.

children.heading: Air traffic services initial data report.—
children.label: 1 Air traffic services initial data report.—
i
children.text: The total costs allocable to the use of air traffic services for that segment during such fiscal year.

children.label: ii
children.text: The total revenues received from that segment during such fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Review and determination
children.label: i .—Review and determination
children.text: Not later than 3 months after completion of the initial report required under subparagraph (A), the inspector general of the Department of Transportation shall review and determine the validity of the model used by the Administrator and the Chief Operating Officer to complete the calculations required under subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: the inspector general shall provide the Administrator and Chief Operating Officer recommendations on how to revise the model;

children.label: II
children.text: the Administrator and the Chief Operating Officer shall complete the calculations required by subparagraph (A) utilizing the revised model and resubmit the revised initial report required under subparagraph (A) to the inspector general; and

children.label: III
children.text: not later than 3 months after completion of the revised initial report required under subparagraph (A), the inspector general shall review and determine the validity of the revised model used by the Administrator and the Chief Operating Officer to complete the calculations required by subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Access to data
children.label: iii .—Access to data
children.text: The Administrator and the Chief Operating Officer shall provide the inspector general of the Department of Transportation with unfettered access to all data produced by the cost accounting system operated and maintained pursuant to subsection (e).

children.heading: .—Report to congress
children.label: C .—Report to congress
children.text: Not later than 60 days after completion of the review and receiving a determination that the model used is valid under subparagraph (B), the Administrator and the Chief Operating Officer shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Finance of the Senate a report describing the results of the calculations completed under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Publication
children.label: D .—Publication
children.text: Not later than 60 days after submission of the report required under subparagraph (C), the Administrator and Chief Operating Officer shall publish the initial report, including any revision thereto if required as a result of the validation process for the model.

children.heading: Air traffic services biennial data reporting.—
children.label: 2 Air traffic services biennial data reporting.—
i
children.text: The total costs allocable to the use of the air traffic services for that segment.

children.label: ii
children.text: The total revenues received from that segment.

children.heading: .—Report to congress
children.label: B .—Report to congress
children.text: Not later than 15 days after completing the calculations under subparagraph (A), the Administrator and the Chief Operating Officer shall complete and submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Finance of the Senate a report containing the results of such calculations.

children.heading: .—Publication
children.label: C .—Publication
children.text: Not later than 60 days after completing the calculations pursuant to subparagraph (A), the Administrator and the Chief Operating Officer shall publish the results of such calculations.

children.heading: Segments of air traffic services users.—
children.label: 3 Segments of air traffic services users.—
i
children.text: Passenger air carriers conducting operations under part 121 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.label: ii
children.text: All-cargo air carriers conducting operations under part 121 of such title.

children.label: iii
children.text: Operators covered by part 125 of such title.

children.label: iv
children.text: Air carriers and operators of piston-engine aircraft operating under part 135 of such title.

children.label: v
children.text: Air carriers and operators of turbine-engine aircraft operating under part 135 of such title.

children.label: vi
children.text: Foreign air carriers providing passenger air transportation.

children.label: vii
children.text: Foreign air carriers providing all-cargo air transportation.

children.label: viii
children.text: Operators of turbine-engine aircraft operating under part 91 of such title, excluding those operating under subpart (K) of such part.

children.label: ix
children.text: Operators of piston-engine aircraft operating under part 91 of such title, excluding those operating under subpart (K) of such part.

children.label: x
children.text: Operators covered by subpart (K) of part 91 of such title.

children.label: xi
children.text: Operators covered by part 133 of such title.

children.label: xii
children.text: Operators covered by part 136 of such title.

children.label: xiii
children.text: Operators covered by part 137 of such title.

children.label: xiv
children.text: Operators of public aircraft that qualify under section 40125.

children.label: xv
children.text: Operators of aircraft that neither take off from, nor land in, the United States.

children.heading: .—Additional segments
children.label: B .—Additional segments
children.text: The Secretary may identify and include additional segments of air traffic users under subparagraph (A) as revenue and air traffic services cost data become available for that additional segment of air traffic services users.

children.label: i
children.text: used for the monitoring, directing, control, and guidance of aircraft or flows of aircraft and for the safe conduct of flight, including communications, navigation, and surveillance services and provision of aeronautical information; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provided directly, or contracted for, by the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: .—Air traffic services user
children.label: B .—Air traffic services user
children.text: The term “air traffic services user” means any individual or entity using air traffic services provided directly, or contracted for, by the Federal Aviation Administration within United States airspace or international airspace delegated to the United States.
